content.full_text:
State of California Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-3660                   322-5901            322-6441            322-6444

May 18, 1978


Mr. William F. Wilson
District Superintendent
Pierce Jt. Unified School District
Anaheim, California  95912

Dear Mr. Wilson:

     This is in response to your letter of April 27, 1978, to Michael Bennett, in which you expressed your district's understanding that school district board members are exempt from filing Statements of Economic Interests.

     Under the Political Reform Act, Government Code §81000, et seq., school districts are specifically listed among those agencies which are required to adopt Conflict of Interest Codes.  Section 87300 provides in part that "Every agency shall adopt and promulgate a Conflict of Interest Code...".  The term "agency" is defined in §82003 to include "... any ... local government agency...".  Section 82041 defines "local government agency" to include "a district of any kind including school district...".

     The required provisions of a Conflict of Interest Code are specified in §87302.  Subsection (b), in particular, provides that each Code shall contain provisions that each employee designated therein shall be required to file Statements of Economic Interests.  Thus, under the foregoing sections, it is clear that school board members are required to file Statements of Economic Interests pursuant to a Conflict of Interest Code adopted by the school district.

William E. Wilson
May 18, 1978
Page Two


     As noted in our letter to your district dated March 31,
1978, we have not yet received a copy of your district's
Code. We again request that your district take steps to
adopt a Code and send a copy to our office for approval.
I have enclosed copies of two model Conflict of Interest
Codes whose format have been adopted by most school
districts for whom the PPPC is Code reviewing body. We
would be happy to assist you in developing a Code for your
district by providing technical assistance if you so desire.

     Thank you for your cooperation.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [illegible]

                                        Ted Prim, Chief
                                        Conflicts of Interest Division

Pierce Joint Unified School District

District Superintendent
William E. Wilson
Pierce High School
William E. Wilson, Principal
Gregg LeMaster, Vice-Principal
Arbuckle Elementary
Thomas F. Hamilton, Principal
Dunnigan Elementary
Carolyn Reiff, Principal
Grand Island Elementary
Ivan J. Hansen, Principal

ARBUCKLE, CALIFORNIA 95912 '78
(916) 475-2852

Trustees
President
Jim Kaltsbeek
Vice-President
Gregory Ramos
Clerk
Charles E. Kelley, Jr.
Members
John S. Abele
John Miller

APR 1978

Mr. Michael Bennett, Executive Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California    95804

Dear Mr. Bennett:

     It has been our understanding that the board members of school
districts are exempt from filing economic interest statements.

     However, the board members on the above letterhead have no
economic interest that conflicts with school business, nor
do I.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        William E. Wilson
                                        District Superintendent

sections.question: Are school district board members exempt from filing Statements of Economic Interests?
sections.conclusion: Under the Political Reform Act, Government Code §82000, et seq., school districts are specifically listed as those agencies which are required to adopt Conflict of Interest Codes. Section 87300 provides that every agency shall adopt and promulgate a Conflict of Interest Code. The term 'agency' is defined in §82003 to include 'any... local government agency...' and §82041 defines 'local government agency' to include 'a district of any kind including school district...'. Section 87302(b) requires that each Code shall contain provisions requiring designated officials to file Statements of Economic Interests.
sections.question_synthetic: The district superintendent seeks clarification on whether school district board members are required to file Statements of Economic Interests under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: School district board members are not exempt from filing Statements of Economic Interests. School districts must adopt a Conflict of Interest Code designating which positions are required to file such statements, as mandated by the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both incoming request (superintendent's letter dated April 27, 1978) and FPPC response (May 18, 1978). Extracted primary response from FPPC letter. Some OCR damage apparent in original document but key content is legible. FPPC references prior letter from March 31, 1978 indicating this is follow-up correspondence.

QUESTION: Are school district board members exempt from filing Statements of Economic Interests?

CONCLUSION: Under the Political Reform Act, Government Code §82000, et seq., school districts are specifically listed as those agencies which are required to adopt Conflict of Interest Codes. Section 87300 provides that every agency shall adopt and promulgate a Conflict of Interest Code. The term 'agency' is defined in §82003 to include 'any... local government agency...' and §82041 defines 'local government agency' to include 'a district of any kind including school district...'. Section 87302(b) requires that each Code shall contain provisions requiring designated officials to file Statements of Economic Interests.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to an inquiry from a school district superintendent regarding whether school board members are exempt from filing Statements of Economic Interests, clarifying that school districts are required agencies under the Political Reform Act to adopt Conflict of Interest Codes that designate filing requirements.
